methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GHGSat is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Stephane Germain that has raised $67M+. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs, though narrow-filter peer matching proved insufficient for direct application.

Only one triangulation method is applicable here. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $67M total raised as a proxy for $10M base value, then applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. This method reflects the step-up pattern observed across early-stage space ventures. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint for private company valuation. Peer-median analysis is similarly inapplicable; while 23 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy minimum thresholds for same-stage or adjacent-stage matching at comparable valuation tiers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to public-market Space & Aerospace multiples (median 45.3x P/S), and path-to-profitability. The valuation is notably sensitive to the quality and timeliness of future disclosed financial metrics.
